name: The Credit Agents
description_short: Houston-based credit repair company using pay-per-deletion pricing. Disputes errors and negative items with all three bureaus; assigns a dedicated credit analyst per client.
description_long:
The Credit Agents LLC is a Houston, Texas-based credit repair company founded in 2012 by Joe Chavarria. Operating out of 11211 Katy Fwy, they serve clients nationwide who need negative items disputed and removed from their credit reports. The company is not BBB accredited and the BBB has not issued a rating due to insufficient information. No CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC certifications have been verified from their website or public records.

The company's core offering is credit dispute work across all three major bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — using consumer protection statutes including the FDCPA to challenge inaccurate or unverifiable negative items. Their dispute catalog is broad, covering third-party collections, debt buyer accounts, medical collections, charge-offs, tax liens, broken leases, repossessions, judgments, foreclosures, bankruptcies, and identity fraud entries. Beyond bureau disputes, they offer a mortgage ambassador service pairing clients with in-house home buyer consultants, plus ongoing credit mentoring aimed at sustaining score gains after the program ends.

The most distinctive feature of The Credit Agents is their pay-per-deletion pricing structure — clients pay per successfully removed item rather than a flat monthly subscription. This aligns the company's financial incentive with client outcomes. A one-time setup fee of approximately $349 is charged upfront. Each client is assigned a dedicated credit analyst, gains access to an FDCPA attorney network for complex or stubborn items, and can monitor progress through a 24/7 online portal. The program is designed to complete within six months, and the company offers a conditional warranty: clients who complete six months as active participants and receive zero deleted items may request a full refund. With a 4.7/5 rating drawn from roughly 2,755 reviews across third-party platforms, customer satisfaction is well-documented at scale.

The pay-per-deletion model is genuinely consumer-friendly in theory — you only pay for results — but the exact per-deletion fee schedule is not publicly disclosed on the website, requiring direct contact before clients can estimate total program cost. The ~$349 setup fee is a real upfront commitment regardless of outcomes. The BBB's inability to rate the company is a transparency gap worth noting, though it does not by itself indicate complaints. The six-month warranty is meaningful but conditional: it only applies to clients who receive zero deletions, meaning partial results don't trigger any refund. Clients with only one or two disputed items may find per-deletion pricing more expensive than a flat-fee competitor.

pricing.guarantee_details: 6-month conditional warranty: clients who complete 6 months as active participants and receive zero deleted items may request a full refund. Partial results do not qualify.

pros: Pay-per-deletion model — clients only pay when items are actually removed from their report
4.7/5 rating from approximately 2,755 reviews across third-party platforms
6-month money-back warranty if zero items are deleted after completing the program
Works with all three major bureaus: Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion
Dedicated credit analyst assigned to each client for personalized dispute management
FDCPA attorney network available for complex items like bankruptcies and judgments
Free initial credit review with no obligation to enroll
cons: ~$349 setup fee required upfront before any dispute work begins, regardless of eventual results
Per-deletion pricing schedule is not publicly disclosed — total program cost is unpredictable without a direct quote
Six-month warranty is conditional on zero deletions; clients with partial results receive no refund
Not BBB accredited; BBB has insufficient information to issue a rating, a transparency gap for cautious consumers
No verified industry certifications (NACSO, NFCC, HUD) found on website or in public records
best_for: Consumers with multiple removable negative items who prefer paying only for verified deletions
Prospective home buyers needing credit repair combined with mortgage qualification guidance
People with complex derogatory items such as bankruptcies, judgments, or charge-offs requiring attorney-backed disputes
Individuals with credit scores in the 400–600 range actively working toward mortgage eligibility
diagnosis: The Credit Agents is best suited for consumers with several disputed negative items who want a results-tied payment model and are comfortable with an upfront setup fee. The in-house mortgage ambassador service makes it particularly relevant for buyers working toward home loan qualification. The main caveat is cost opacity — without knowing the per-deletion rate or the number of removable items, the total program cost cannot be estimated before enrolling.
services: Identity fraud and inaccurate personal information disputes
Home loan application assistance (mortgage ambassador service)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
